title: BEA News Releases - Index of Current and Recent Releases
summary: This page is a listing index of U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) news releases, not the releases themselves. It enumerates recent economic data publications including GDP estimates, personal income and outlays, and international trade figures. The page contains no substantive data, figures, or analysis—only release titles and publication dates. Each entry must be accessed individually for actual macro catalyst data.
key_points: U.S. International Transactions and Investment Position for Q4 and Year 2025 published March 25, 2026
Personal Income and Outlays for January 2026 published March 13, 2026
GDP (Second Estimate) for Q4 and Year 2025 published March 13, 2026
U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services for January 2026 published March 12, 2026
Outdoor Recreation Economic Statistics for 2024 published March 5, 2026
GDP by State and Personal Income by State for Q3 2025 published January 23, 2026
why_it_matters: This index identifies recent BEA releases that represent macro catalysts. Key releases include GDP estimates, personal income/outlays (which feed into PCE inflation analysis), and international trade data—all of which are market-moving economic indicators. However, the actual catalyst data must be retrieved from individual release pages.
macro_context: The BEA releases listed include high-frequency indicators (monthly personal income/outlays, trade data) and periodic estimates (quarterly and annual GDP). These releases are standard macro catalysts that affect markets when published individually. This listing page provides no data values, only temporal reference points for the releases.
market_structure_context: BEA releases are scheduled economic announcements that create predictable volatility windows in financial markets. This index does not contain data relevant to market structure analysis.

notes: WEAK SOURCE: This page is an index of BEA releases, not the releases themselves. The content is limited to 183 words listing only release titles and dates with no data values. Each listed release (GDP estimates, personal income/outlays, trade data) would constitute a separate, more substantive macro catalyst record if archived individually. This record is suitable only as a temporal index of BEA release activity.
